error.signature: GitHub webhook delivers same event multiple times because endpoint is slow or returns non-2xx

verdict.summary: GitHub webhooks timeout after 10 seconds and retry failed deliveries. There's no configurable retry policy. Slow endpoints receive duplicate deliveries. The X-GitHub-Delivery header is unique per delivery attempt, not per event.

dead_ends.action: Process webhook synchronously and return 200 after completion
dead_ends.why_fails: If processing takes >10s, GitHub considers the delivery failed and retries. You get duplicate processing of the same event.

dead_ends.action: Use X-GitHub-Delivery header as idempotency key
dead_ends.why_fails: X-GitHub-Delivery is unique per delivery ATTEMPT, not per event. Retries have different delivery IDs. Use the event payload's action+id for dedup.

workarounds.action: Return 200 immediately and process webhook asynchronously
workarounds.how: Enqueue the raw webhook payload for background processing. Return 200 within 1 second.

workarounds.action: Use event ID from payload (not delivery header) for idempotency
workarounds.how: Dedup key: f'{event_type}:{payload["action"]}:{payload["id"]}'  # unique per logical event

workarounds.action: Check webhook delivery logs in GitHub repo settings for debugging
workarounds.how: Settings -> Webhooks -> Recent Deliveries shows each attempt with request/response details
